sourceMovie.title: Maverick
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Maverick works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Ballad of Buster Scruggs
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Maverick clicked for you, The Ballad of Buster Scruggs (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: ¡Three Amigos!
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Maverick clicked for you, ¡Three Amigos! (1986) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Legends of the Fall
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Maverick clicked for you, Legends of the Fall (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and western, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Maverick?
faq.answer: Try The Ballad of Buster Scruggs, ¡Three Amigos!, Legends of the Fall—each shares DNA with Maverick while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Maverick?
faq.answer: Maverick (1994) is best described as Action, Adventure, Comedy, Drama, Western. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Maverick worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
